summary: Backend Web Push suite fully passing (10/10 pytest). Frontend PushNotificationButton NOT rendering on Altro tab despite import + usage in altro.tsx and no JS errors — no push-btn-* testID, no 'Notifiche' text in DOM after login as admin (archived=false) via both tab navigation and direct /altro URL.

ui_bugs.issue: Component does NOT render anywhere on the Altro tab. None of testIDs push-btn-subscribe / push-btn-unsubscribe / push-btn-denied / push-btn-unsupported appear in DOM. No 'ATTIVA NOTIFICHE PUSH', 'Notifiche bloccate', or 'Notifiche non supportate' text found. Verified with (1) admin login (archived=false) via tab click AND (2) direct URL navigation to /altro. Total testIDs on Altro = only the 6 grid cards (altro-card-*). No JS errors in console (only style deprecation warnings). Bundle rebuilds fresh (touched altro.tsx). Browser APIs are all available (serviceWorker+PushManager+Notification present, permission='denied'), so the expected fallback should be push-btn-denied card.

action_items: Investigate why PushNotificationButton component does not mount in Altro screen. Suggested checks: (a) add a top-level console.log inside PushNotificationButton to see if it renders at all, (b) verify /app/frontend/src/context/AuthContext exports user with archived===false (confirmed via localStorage: archived:false), (c) check if some ErrorBoundary catches a silent error (the `apiService.getPushStatus()` call succeeds from the browser: 200 {"subscribed":false,"devices":0}), (d) check that Metro isn't serving a stale bundle for this route (a hard restart of the frontend supervisor may be needed if hot reload didn't pick up the new component file — try `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend`), (e) verify /app/frontend/src/services/api.ts export path is consistent with the import in PushNotificationButton (`from '../services/api'`).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: send_push_to_all_users uses .to_list(5000) — cap is documented, but for growing user bases (>5k subs) the loop silently drops the rest. Consider paginating or using a background job (Celery).

server.py: cleanup only removes subs on 404/410. Other failures (403 auth revoked, 400) are counted as `failed` but never cleaned; if a large fraction goes bad it can slow down every announcement send. Add a longer-term staleness cleanup.

The push broadcast is fully synchronous inside the create_announcement/toggle handler; with hundreds of subscribers a 10s timeout per call could exceed HTTP request limits. Should be dispatched as a background task (FastAPI BackgroundTasks or asyncio.create_task).

PushNotificationButton.tsx: `subscribed` UI state does not sync back to Notification.permission changes if user revokes permission from browser settings; consider a periodic re-check or a visibilitychange listener.
test_credentials: Admin danielebrufani@gmail.com / Mariavittoria23 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). testclient_kpi@test.com is stuck on a mandatory '7 giorni di prova' activation gate — cannot access tabs until activated; use admin for UI checks or activate the trial for the test client.
seed_data_creation: None persisted (all test announcements and push subscriptions cleaned up in teardown).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend endpoints fully covered in /app/backend/tests/test_web_push.py (10 tests, all green). Frontend blocker: PushNotificationButton absent from DOM on Altro. Backend API /api/push/status returned 200 from within the browser after admin login, so it's a pure frontend rendering bug. When retesting after fix, jump straight to admin login + click 'Altro' tab and look for [data-testid='push-btn-denied'] (chromium headless has Notification.permission='denied' by default) or push-btn-subscribe if permission is 'default'.
rca of the issue: Symptom: PushNotificationButton mounts nowhere on Altro. Reproduction: login as admin danielebrufani@gmail.com → click Altro tab (or navigate to /altro) → scroll to bottom → no push button. Verified: (1) file imported correctly at altro.tsx L18, used at L178 within `!isArchived && <View><PushNotificationButton /></View>`; (2) user.archived === false (from localStorage user object); (3) no JS runtime errors; (4) apiService.getPushStatus() succeeds from browser console with 200 body {subscribed:false,devices:0}; (5) Notification API + serviceWorker + PushManager all present in Playwright chromium (Notification.permission === 'denied' → should trigger push-btn-denied render path). Suspected root cause: silent bundle issue OR the useEffect / apiService import path being resolved to an undefined at build-time. Recommend main agent add a debug console.log('[PushBtn] render', {isSupported, checking, permission}) at the top of PushNotificationButton and reload to isolate whether the component function is called at all.
